raw_consumer_statement:
I had multiple unauthorized charges on my American Express account, including one from XXXX. Amex denied my fraud dispute by claiming the XXXX policy was in my namehowever, this alone does not prove authorization. 

A fraudster can easily open accounts using stolen identity information. Amex failed to provide any actual documentation, did not determine if the transaction was authorized by me, and ignored the legal standards for a reasonable investigation under Regulation Z ( 12 CFR 1026.13 ).

Instead, Amex abruptly closed my account without warning and refused to reopen it despite over 25 attempts to resolve this by phone. They did not give me access to their findings or allow me to appeal the decision.

I have never received a proper written explanation of their denial, and the claim was dismissed without a reasonable basis. This violates the Fair Credit Billing Act and my consumer rights.
